zh.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston於1266年出生在蘇格蘭。他和Gilchrist Mure至少有1個兒子。他去世於1296年在他的家鄉享年30歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston于1266年出生在苏格兰。他和Gilchrist Mure至少有1个儿子。他去世于1296年在他的家乡享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Danielston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Mauritz, Nels, Nils, Sigfrid, Alf, Arlys, Berent, Bjorn, Eskil, Gunner.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Danielsson: patronymic from the personal name Daniel .
Americanized form of Norwegian, Danish, and North German   Danielsen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Daniel
meanings: English, French, Spanish, Portuguese, German, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Romanian, Jewish, Assyrian/Chaldean, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Breton (rarely Le Daniel); Hungarian (Dániel): from the Biblical personal name Daniel, Syriac (Assyrian/Chaldean) Dānīʾēl, Hungarian Dániel (from Hebrew Daniyyel ‘God is my judge’), borne by a major prophet in the Bible. The chief factor influencing the popularity of the personal name (and hence the frequency of the surname) was undoubtedly the dramatic story in the Book of Daniel recounting the prophet's steadfast adherence to his religious faith despite pressure and persecution from the Mesopotamian kings in whose court he served: Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, and Darius. The name was also borne by a 2nd-century Christian martyr and by a 9th-century hermit, the legend of whose lives were popular among Christians during the Middle Ages. Among Orthodox Christians in Eastern Europe the name was also popular as being that of a 4th-century Persian martyr, who was venerated in the Orthodox Church. In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.

Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of McDaniel , which is actually a variant of McDonnell , from the Gaelic form of Irish Donal (equivalent to Scottish Donald), erroneously associated with the Biblical personal name Daniel. See also O'Donnell .
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Danijel: from the Biblical personal name Danijel ‘Daniel’ (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston was born in 1266, in Scotland. He had at least 1 son with Gilchrist Mure. He died in 1296, in his hometown, at the age of 30.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston est né en 1266, à Écosse. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gilchrist Mure. Il est décédé en 1296, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 30 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston wurde 1266 in Scotland geboren. Er hatte mit Gilchrist Mure mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1296 im Alter von 30 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston è nato il nel 1266 nel Scotland. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gilchrist Mure. Morì nel 1296, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 30 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielstonは1266年にスコットランドで生まれた。彼はGilchrist Mureとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1296年に故郷で30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston(은)는 1266년 스컷랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 Gilchrist Mure(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1296년 그의 고향에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston nasceu em 1266, em Scotland. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Gilchrist Mure. Ele faleceu em 1296, em sua cidade natal, com 30 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston родился в 1266 г. в местности Шотландия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gilchrist Mure. Он умер в 1296 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 30 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh de Danielston nació en 1266, en Scotland. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Gilchrist Mure. Murió en 1296, en su pueblo, a la edad de 30 años.
